summary: ai-sdk-helpers exfiltrates developer identity on install to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js silently harvests the installing developer's identity and ships it to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/ — a destination unrelated to the package's claimed homepage (ai-sdk.guide). The script reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config, the project's.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (the GitHub CLI auth/host file) to extract git email and GitHub login/email, and combines those with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), CI provider detection, and the GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL / GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL / EMAIL environment variables. The combined payload is POSTed via https.request at install time without user consent. Loading ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml in full into memory also places the gh OAuth token within reach of any future variant, even though only login/email substrings are exfiltrated in this version. The package name `ai-sdk-helpers` and the README's 'Production utilities for Vercel AI SDK' framing impersonate the Vercel AI SDK namespace (`ai`, `@ai-sdk/*`); the publisher 'AI SDK Guide <hello@ai-sdk.guide>' is unaffiliated with Vercel. The result is a typosquat-with-payload that targets developers working with the AI SDK and collects identifiers suitable for follow-on phishing or account-targeting.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
